extraction.one_line_summary: VEMOSE Alexis won from a 5-man breakaway that survived by functioning as disciplined time-trialling unit, holding 30 seconds over disorganized peloton on final climb.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo's Skujins isolated doing chase work while others refused, revealing lack of intermediate stage commitment from GC teams.
5-man breakaway survived final categorized climb with only 30-second margin because they time-trialled cooperatively while peloton argued about workrate.
Wout van Aert defended yellow with minimal effort, taking 6th from breakaway group despite modest time gaps showing comfort gambling.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: tagged off breakaway again and shed 7+ minutes, revealing poor climbing form even on lumpy terrain
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: negative: withdrew mid-stage, indicating Dauphine taking physical toll on sprinters
rider_signals.VEMOSE Alexis: positive: 34-year-old veteran showed elite finishing speed from breakaway, winning by bike length after cooperative riding
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: controlled yellow jersey defensively from breakaway group rather than defending from peloton, revealing confidence in small time gaps
